desc: The /add endpoint accepts missing, null, and non-string items, causing later /items requests to fail in len(), slicing, or upper().
Unsynchronized mutable globals allow concurrent add and reset requests to lose updates and make DATA_STORE and USER_COUNT inconsistent.
Process-local in-memory storage produces inconsistent data and counts across multiple workers and loses all data on restart.
Resetting data permanently changes CONFIG mode to reset, unexpectedly altering all subsequent /items processing from truncation to uppercasing.
Running the application with debug=True can expose sensitive debugging information and an interactive debugger if used in a reachable deployment.
The /add endpoint returns raw exception messages to clients, potentially disclosing internal implementation details.
The /add exception handler returns an error body with the default HTTP 200 status, preventing clients and monitoring from reliably detecting failures.
Catching every Exception in /add masks programming and infrastructure failures instead of handling only expected request-validation errors.
The unauthenticated /add endpoint permits unlimited items and item sizes, allowing memory exhaustion through repeated or oversized requests.
A very long digit-only param can make int(param) raise ValueError due to Python's integer-string conversion limit, yielding an unhandled 500 response.
